_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Routine post-operative follow-up after VVF repair surgery, typically after a week.
Assessment of healing and progress post-surgery.
Evaluation of vesical integrity and readiness for catheter removal.

preauth.label: Clinical notes of current visit detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for follow-up procedure
Discharge Summary of last admission for VVF repair surgery

claim.label: Detailed clinical notes of current visit and treatment given
Post procedure USG / UFM / Urine Routine report

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes of current visit with detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for follow-up procedure submitted?
Was the Discharge Summary of last admission for VVF repair surgery submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed clinical notes of the current visit and treatment given submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes of current visit are missing or do not detail history, signs & symptoms, or indication for follow-up procedure at pre-authorization.
Discharge summary of the last admission for VVF repair surgery is missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Detailed clinical notes of the current visit and treatment given are missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Post-procedure USG, UFM, or Urine Routine report is missing or incomplete at claim submission.
Clinical notes and/or discharge summary of last admission for VVF repair surgery are not indicative of a follow-up procedure.

clinical_key_pointers: VVF is a major cause of concern.
Simple fistulas are usually small in size (≤0.5cm) and are present as single non radiated fistulas.
Complex fistulas include previously failed fistula repairs or large-sized (≥2.5 cm) fistulas, more often a result of chronic diseases.
VVFs causes continual wetness, Odor, and discomfort cause serious social problems.
VVF repair is a subtype of female urogenital fistula (UGF).
VVF is an abnormal fistulous tract extending between the bladder and the vagina that allows the continuous involuntary discharge of urine into the vaginal vault.
Guidelines to follow intraoperatively to minimize VVF formation are followed during VVF Repair surgery.
The postoperative management is of vital importance. Follow-up visit: Usually after a week of the VVF repair surgery.
additional_information.post_op_care: The bladder should remain catheterized for 2–3 weeks after repair. Follow-up post-operative advice given to patient i.e. Minimizing Valsalva maneuvers and examine any infection and progress post-surgery. The key to postoperative management is the maintenance of a dry, uninfected suture line. Post-operative precautions to avoid damage due to delicate tissue. The urethral catheter may be discontinued on the fifth to seventh day. If vesical integrity is noted 2 weeks later on a cystogram, the suprapubic catheter may be removed. Surgeries to repair pelvic radiotherapy associated VVFs require longer periods of drainage.
